methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $60M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Tenna Systems is a Seed-stage defense and security company founded by Avner and Gabriel Bendheim, currently valued at $60M post-money on $13.5M raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 10 private companies and 2 public entities operating in Defense & Security, including peers such as Anduril Industries, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the current $60M post-money valuation, applies a typical 2.5x multiplier for Seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying a $150M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, and Ares Industries at $150M. The median of this set is $300M, establishing a range of $150M to $500M.

Triangulating these two methods across their midpoints and bounds yields a valuation range of $128M (low) to $345M (high), with a median estimate of $225M. The wide range reflects meaningful uncertainty around comparable company selection and stage-based multiplier assumptions in an early-stage, capital-intensive sector. Key sensitivities include the timing and terms of the next funding round, which would directly inform stage-progression methodology, and the sustainability of peer valuations in a volatile defense-tech market. The current $60M valuation sits below the implied low end, suggesting potential upside if execution proceeds as anticipated.
